title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. coccinea
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. coccinea, also known as the Red Helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits associated with Red Helleborine include its ability to aid in respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. coccinea can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
